observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the interest of the Municipality should at all times be protected, all provisions of law, rules and regulations pertaining to procurement should be strictly adhered to. We further recommended that: 

A comprehensive Annual Procurement Plan should be prepared based on the Annual Budget. No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP; 

The Municipal Engineer should monitor and inspect all completed projects during the one (1) year defects liability period to determine any defects for correction by the concerned contractors and to issue the Final Certificate of Acceptance after one year from project completion date and to require the concerned contractor to post the required warranty bond in accordance with Section 62.2.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: 1.7 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Contractors to provide Statement of Work Accomplished or Progress Billings before the year-end, which will serve as the basis of the Accounting Office in recognizing the appropriate CIP and Liability accounts. Also, we recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all on-going infrastructure projects are recognized as CIP in the year-end financial statements.

2.5 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the semi-expendable properties and the related Accumulated Depreciation to the appropriate accounts and thereafter follow the new capitalization threshold of newly acquired PPE and other guidelines in the issuance of semi-expendable items. Also, issue the corresponding ICS to establish accountability over these tangible items which were already issued to the end-users. If it is unlocated/missing/lost, determine the concerned personnel liable for the loss and require them to pay for the cost of the property since the required request for accountability had not been filed within the prescribed period.

3.5 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to present properly the SCBAA in order to have an accurate and complete financial information and to present fairly the financial statements at year-end pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS 24.

4.10 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with IPSAS 1, 2, and 17, COA Circular No. 2015-008, and NGAS Manual for LGUs by providing adequate, simple and brief narrative description on restricted cash balances, fully depreciated movable PPE and Local Road Network to ensure that pertinent information are available to intended users of the financial statements for good decision-making.

5.8 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation in accordance with the IPSAS and existing COA issuances and make proper adjustments in the related/affected accounts.

6.5 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books collections of discounts on prompt payments of RPT and SET during the year in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements at year-end.

7.3 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant record only the expired portion of the insurance paid as expense and the unexpired portion to be retained as asset.  Further, the following proposed adjusting journal entry should be taken up by the Municipal Accountant for fair presentation of the financial statements:
Prepaid Insurance                                  P120,322.34
Prior Year Adjustment                                                          P120322.34
To record unexpired portion of insurance paid.

8.5 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer that all collections should be deposited intact daily with authorized depository bank as prescribed by the provisions of Section 69(1) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS, Vol. I for LGUs in order to avoid possible misuse or misappropriation of government funds.

9.7 We recommended that the Management cause the amount of P1,788,056.17 to be refunded by the Contractor. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer/Project Monitoring Committee should strengthen their monitoring to ensure that projects are satisfactorily accomplished before payment in order to avoid disallowance.

10.6 We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation of construction materials distributed by the Municipality to various recipients. Require the recipients to submit project status reports, acknowledgment receipts of the materials, certification that the construction materials were used for the intended purpose as per POW, and pictures.  This is to establish a sound internal control on the distribution of construction materials pursuant to PD No. 1445.

11.8 We recommended that the Management cause strict compliance  to COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the timely submission of copies of all perfected government contracts, POs and all its supporting documents for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects within five days upon execution/issuance in order to conduct timely evaluation and review of the regularity and propriety of the contracts.

12.6 We recommended that the Management instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
maintain a special account in the GF for the 20% MDF and Tobacco Excise Tax Fund and separate subsidiary ledgers for the accounts to ensure that monitoring of the funds shall be done in a manner that shall be beneficial in the decision-making process of the LGU; and
ensure that the cash for the 20% MDF and Tobacco Excise Tax Fund shall have separate reporting and monitoring since the funds are available exclusively for the specific purpose for which they have been created.
Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the opening of a separate depository accounts and cashbooks for the 20% MDF and Tobacco Excise Tax Fund in order to determine the cash balances at any given period.

13.6 We recommended that the Management instruct the implementing office to distribute the remaining farm equipment to intended beneficiaries to avoid deterioration and damaged of the same. Future similar transactions should be distributed immediately after inspection of the procured items in order for the intended beneficiaries to maximize the benefits that could be derived from the usage thereof.

14.5 We recommended that the Management require the GAD Focal Point to ensure the effective implementation and monitoring of the LGUs PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget so that the benefits expected from these PPAs will be timely gained by the Municipality’s constituents.

15.5 We recommended that the Management instruct the concerned Office to fast-tract the connection of electricity on the installed CCTV Cameras in various location of the Municipality in order not to deprive its constituents and even commuters of the benefits which could be derived from the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit to the COA Auditor all DVs for CY 2023 totalling P196,794,614.52 pursuant to the provisions of Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006  and Section 5.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 7, 1997 to give the Auditor enough time to examine and audit all transactions and  request for inspection and evaluation by a COA TAS on completed infrastructure projects.

Municipal Treasurer, as an accountable officer to uphold with honesty, dignity and god fearing in the performance of her duties and responsibilities as prescribed under the constitution; and
remind the Officers and Employees of the Municipality to act/perform in   accordance with the constitution which provides:
2 Public office is a public trust. Public officers and employees must, at all times, be accountable to the people, serve them with utmost responsibility, integrity, loyalty, and efficiency; act with patriotism and justice, and lead modest lives2
